channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Microsoft, Accenture and Movement to Work Launch Free Gen AI Training for Jobseekers
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Young people exploring their career paths can now access a valuable digital learning opportunity through the Gen AI for Jobseekers training program. Developed through a partnership between Microsoft, Accenture, and the nonprofit Movement to Work, the program helps young jobseekers learn how to use generative AI responsibly and confidently during their job search. This free […]

The post Microsoft, Accenture and Movement to Work Launch Free Gen AI Training for Jobseekers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people exploring their career paths can now access a valuable digital learning opportunity through the Gen AI for Jobseekers training program. Developed through a partnership between Microsoft, Accenture, and the nonprofit Movement to Work, the program helps young jobseekers learn how to use generative AI responsibly and confidently during their job search.

This free online course provides practical guidance on using artificial intelligence tools to explore career opportunities, improve job applications, and prepare for interviews. It is designed to help young people develop the digital skills needed to navigate today’s technology-driven job market.

The training is self-paced and includes short videos, practical exercises, and interactive activities that allow learners to practice using AI tools in real-world career scenarios. Participants also gain access to an AI Copilot tool that assists them in developing job-search strategies and refining their applications.

What Participants Will Learn

The Gen AI for Jobseekers training covers several essential topics designed to support young people as they begin their professional journey.

Key areas include:

  • Exploring different career paths and job roles

  • Tailoring CVs and job applications using AI tools

  • Practicing interview questions and responses

  • Planning next career steps with the help of AI

These modules are designed to make AI tools accessible while emphasizing safe and responsible use.

Program Features

Participants in the course benefit from a variety of engaging learning elements, including:

  • Self-paced learning modules with short instructional videos

  • Hands-on activities designed to build practical AI skills

  • An integrated AI Copilot tool for job-search support

  • A digital certificate upon successful completion of the course

These features allow learners to build confidence while gaining valuable digital competencies.

Real-World Adoption

The program is already being integrated into youth employment initiatives in the United Kingdom. It is currently used within work experience programs run by the Department for Work and Pensions under the GOV.UK platform.

The training is also being introduced across Youth Hubs and Job Centres, helping ensure that young people have access to high-quality digital skills support regardless of their location.

Why This Training Matters

As artificial intelligence becomes increasingly important in recruitment and job searching, young jobseekers need the knowledge and confidence to use these tools effectively.

The Gen AI for Jobseekers training empowers participants with the skills to:

  • Navigate AI-powered job application tools

  • Improve their chances during the recruitment process

  • Build digital literacy for the modern workplace

Employers, educators, and youth organizations are encouraged to share this resource widely to help more young people benefit from the program.

How to Access the Training

The training is freely available online and can be accessed through the official learning platform.Learn more and start the course.

For more training courses from OFY, visit here.

The post Microsoft, Accenture and Movement to Work Launch Free Gen AI Training for Jobseekers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Exciting Climate Change Project Coordinator Jobs 2026 (Remote – Canada)
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
ICLEI Canada is currently hiring two Climate Change Project Coordinators to join its Adaptation Team and support climate resilience initiatives across Canada. These roles involve coordinating climate adaptation projects, designing workshops, delivering webinars, and developing capacity-building resources to strengthen climate resilience in communities. The positions offer flexible remote work within Canada, making them ideal for […]

The post Exciting Climate Change Project Coordinator Jobs 2026 (Remote – Canada) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
ICLEI Canada is currently hiring two Climate Change Project Coordinators to join its Adaptation Team and support climate resilience initiatives across Canada.

These roles involve coordinating climate adaptation projects, designing workshops, delivering webinars, and developing capacity-building resources to strengthen climate resilience in communities.

The positions offer flexible remote work within Canada, making them ideal for professionals experienced in climate policy, project coordination, and environmental sustainability.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the positions are filled.

About ICLEI Canada

ICLEI Canada is a nonprofit organization that works with municipalities and partners to advance sustainable development, climate adaptation, and resilience-building initiatives.

Through research, training programs, and policy support, ICLEI helps communities develop strategies to respond to climate change and environmental challenges.

Position Overview

The Climate Change Project Coordinator will play a key role in managing and implementing climate adaptation initiatives.

Responsibilities include:

  • Designing and delivering capacity-building workshops
  • Organizing and facilitating webinars and training programs
  • Supporting project coordination and implementation
  • Developing tools and educational resources
  • Collaborating with project partners and stakeholders
  • Supporting climate adaptation initiatives across Canada

The position requires strong communication, coordination, and problem-solving skills in a fast-paced project environment.

Key Requirements

Applicants should have:

  • Minimum 4 years of relevant professional experience
  • Experience in project coordination or climate-related work
  • Strong communication and organizational skills
  • Ability to work in remote and collaborative environments
  • Interest in climate adaptation, sustainability, and environmental policy

The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, tech-savvy, and comfortable working on multiple projects simultaneously.

Contract Details

Two positions are available:

1-Year Term Position

  • Full-time position
  • Salary: $70,000 – $85,000
  • Possibility of contract extension

2-Year Term Position

  • Full-time position
  • Salary: $70,000 – $85,000
  • Possibility of contract extension

Both roles allow flexible remote work within Canada.

Project Areas

Selected candidates will support major climate adaptation programs, including:

  • Advancing Implementation Training Initiative (AITI)
  • Ontario Resource Centre for Climate Adaptation (ORCCA)

These initiatives focus on building capacity and resources to help communities implement climate adaptation strategies.

Location

Remote – Canada

Candidates must be able to work remotely within Canada.

Salary

$70,000 – $85,000 per year

Application Process

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the positions are filled.

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interviews.

Applicants from equity-seeking groups, including persons with disabilities, are encouraged to apply.

Official Application Link

Learn more and apply here:

APPLY HERE

For more jobs, fellowships, scholarships, grants, internships and global opportunities, Click here.

The post Exciting Climate Change Project Coordinator Jobs 2026 (Remote – Canada) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Prestigious Marine Biodiversity Conference Grant 2026 in Belgium (Fully Funded Participation)
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
Applications are now open for the World Conference on Marine Biodiversity (WCMB) 2026 Participation Grants, offering financial support for Early Career Ocean Professionals (ECOPs) from the Global South to attend the conference in Belgium. The grants aim to support emerging marine scientists and researchers by providing opportunities to engage with global experts, present research, and […]

The post Prestigious Marine Biodiversity Conference Grant 2026 in Belgium (Fully Funded Participation) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the World Conference on Marine Biodiversity (WCMB) 2026 Participation Grants, offering financial support for Early Career Ocean Professionals (ECOPs) from the Global South to attend the conference in Belgium.

The grants aim to support emerging marine scientists and researchers by providing opportunities to engage with global experts, present research, and contribute to marine biodiversity science and conservation.

The initiative is supported by Ocean Decade Belgium and the VLIZ – Flanders Marine Institute Philanthropy “The Sea as a Good Cause”.

A total of 10 full participation grants worth €2,500 each will be awarded.

Application Deadline: 17 March 2026

About the Conference

The World Conference on Marine Biodiversity (WCMB) is a major international event that brings together researchers, policymakers, and ocean experts to discuss advancements in marine biodiversity science and conservation.

The conference provides a platform for:

  • Scientific collaboration
  • Knowledge exchange
  • Policy discussions
  • Networking among global marine scientists

For early-career researchers, the event offers valuable exposure to international research networks and emerging ocean science initiatives.

Grant Overview

The Belgian National Decade Committee and VLIZ Philanthropy are offering participation grants to help early-career marine scientists attend the conference.

Each selected participant will receive:
Grant Value: €2,500

The funding is intended to support participation costs, including travel and conference expenses.

Number of Grants Available

A total of 10 participation grants will be awarded to ECOPs from the Global South.

The grants are distributed as follows:

  • 4 grants for ECOPs whose supervisors are part of the WCMB scientific committee network
  • 4 grants for ECOPs from institutes collaborating with VLIZ international partners
  • 2 grants for WoRMS editors from the Global South

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be an Early Career Ocean Professional (ECOP)
  • Be under 35 years old
  • Be from the Global South
  • Be enrolled in or affiliated with:
    • BSc programme
    • MSc programme
    • PhD programme
    • Junior postdoctoral position
  • Have an accepted abstract (oral or poster presentation) at WCMB 2026

The selection process will consider:

  • Motivation letter
  • Letter of consent
  • Active conference participation through an accepted abstract

What the Grant Supports

The participation grant allows selected researchers to:

  • Attend the World Conference on Marine Biodiversity 2026
  • Present their research findings
  • Engage with leading marine scientists
  • Expand their international professional network
  • Contribute to global discussions on marine biodiversity conservation

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • Completed application form
  • Motivation letter
  • Letter of consent
  • Proof of accepted oral or poster abstract

Applications must be sent via email to:

filantropie@vliz.be

Additional Opportunities

The WCMB 2026 organizing committee also indicated that additional grant opportunities may become available through partner organizations.

Updates will be shared through the conference website, newsletters, and social media channels.

APPLY HERE

For more scholarships, fellowships, grants and global opportunities, click here

The post Prestigious Marine Biodiversity Conference Grant 2026 in Belgium (Fully Funded Participation) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Exciting Transformative Climate Policy Fellowship 2026 for Indigenous Youth (UNFCCC Participation)
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Re-Earth Initiative Climate Policy Fellowship 2026, a powerful one-year programme designed to support Indigenous youth leaders in participating in international climate negotiations under the UNFCCC. The fellowship equips Indigenous young leaders with the knowledge, technical skills, and networks needed to engage in global climate governance while ensuring that Indigenous […]

The post Exciting Transformative Climate Policy Fellowship 2026 for Indigenous Youth (UNFCCC Participation) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Re-Earth Initiative Climate Policy Fellowship 2026, a powerful one-year programme designed to support Indigenous youth leaders in participating in international climate negotiations under the UNFCCC.

The fellowship equips Indigenous young leaders with the knowledge, technical skills, and networks needed to engage in global climate governance while ensuring that Indigenous perspectives, territorial rights, and traditional knowledge systems are represented in climate policymaking.

The programme runs for one year and includes training sessions, policy development work, and participation in major global climate events, including the UNFCCC Subsidiary Bodies Session (SB 64) and COP 31.

Applications are open 2 March – 16 March 2026 and reviewed on a rolling basis.

About the Fellowship

The Re-Earth Initiative Climate Policy Fellowship for Indigenous Youth was created to strengthen Indigenous representation in global climate decision-making.

Indigenous Peoples are among the communities most impacted by climate change, yet their voices are often excluded from international negotiations.

This fellowship aims to bridge that gap by providing Indigenous youth with opportunities to engage directly in climate diplomacy, policy discussions, and global advocacy efforts.

Participants gain practical experience in international climate negotiations while remaining grounded in their communities’ priorities and lived realities.

Purpose of the Fellowship

The fellowship aims to support Indigenous youth leaders by:

  • Building technical understanding of UNFCCC negotiation processes
  • Strengthening Indigenous leadership in international climate diplomacy
  • Supporting policy engagement rooted in climate justice and territorial defense
  • Encouraging collaboration between Indigenous communities globally
  • Increasing Indigenous representation in global climate forums

The programme emphasizes community accountability and ethical representation, ensuring that participants advocate for climate solutions that reflect Indigenous rights and knowledge systems.

Key Focus Areas

Applicants must select a thematic negotiation track as part of their application.

Available tracks for the 2026 fellowship include:

  • Climate Mitigation
  • Article 6 (Carbon Markets and Cooperative Approaches)
  • Climate Adaptation
  • Climate Finance

Fellows will develop expertise in their selected focus area while also gaining broader knowledge of international climate policy processes.

Programme Structure

The fellowship runs for one year and combines virtual training with in-person participation in global climate events.

Virtual Engagement

Participants will take part in:

  • Virtual training sessions
  • Policy research and brief development
  • Cohort coordination meetings
  • Strategy discussions with other fellows

Training topics include:

  • Climate policy analysis
  • UNFCCC procedures
  • Negotiation literacy
  • Advocacy strategies

International Participation

Fellows will also participate in key international climate negotiation events:

SB 64 – UNFCCC Subsidiary Bodies Session

📅 June 2026

COP 31 – UN Climate Conference

📅 November 2026

These events provide opportunities to observe negotiations, participate in side events, and collaborate with global climate justice networks.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be 18–30 years old
  • Self-identify as Indigenous
  • Demonstrate meaningful connection to their community or Nation
  • Show prior engagement in climate justice, Indigenous rights, territorial defense, or policy advocacy
  • Express strong motivation to participate in international climate negotiations

No formal documentation of Indigenous identity is required, but applicants must demonstrate authentic community connections.

Application Requirements

Applicants must complete the online application form and provide:

  • Personal information and contact details
  • Indigenous People, Nation, or community affiliation
  • Territory or region of engagement
  • Language proficiency level
  • Organizational affiliation (if applicable)
  • Description of relevant experience and motivation

Additionally, applicants must submit:

Letter of Recommendation

  • Maximum two pages
  • PDF format
  • From a community leader, organization, or movement reference

📅 Recommendation Letter Deadline: 21 March 2026

Ideal Candidate Profile

The fellowship seeks applicants who demonstrate:

  • Strong accountability to their Indigenous community
  • Experience in climate justice or advocacy work
  • Interest in international climate governance
  • Ability to collaborate across cultures and regions
  • Commitment to long-term climate justice efforts

Participants are expected to actively engage in training sessions and attend international events.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of the programme, fellows will gain:

  • Strong understanding of UNFCCC negotiation systems
  • Skills in climate policy analysis and advocacy
  • Experience participating in international climate diplomacy
  • Expanded networks within Indigenous and climate justice movements
  • Confidence advocating for rights-based climate solutions

APPLY HERE

For more exciting opportunities including scholarships, fellowships, internships, and grants, click here

The post Exciting Transformative Climate Policy Fellowship 2026 for Indigenous Youth (UNFCCC Participation) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mandela Rhodes Scholarship 2027: Fully Funded Postgraduate Scholarship for African Students to Study in South Africa
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship 2027, one of Africa’s most prestigious leadership and academic scholarship programs. Established to honor the legacy of Nelson Mandela, the scholarship provides exceptional young African leaders with the opportunity to pursue postgraduate studies in South Africa while participating in an intensive leadership development program. The Mandela […]

The post Mandela Rhodes Scholarship 2027: Fully Funded Postgraduate Scholarship for African Students to Study in South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship 2027, one of Africa’s most prestigious leadership and academic scholarship programs. Established to honor the legacy of Nelson Mandela, the scholarship provides exceptional young African leaders with the opportunity to pursue postgraduate studies in South Africa while participating in an intensive leadership development program.

The Mandela Rhodes Scholarship combines comprehensive financial support for postgraduate studies with a transformative leadership program aimed at nurturing a new generation of African leaders who are committed to creating positive change across the continent.

Each year, a select group of young Africans is chosen to become Mandela Rhodes Scholars. The program seeks individuals who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, entrepreneurial spirit, and a strong commitment to reconciliation and social impact.

The scholarship is administered by the Mandela Rhodes Foundation, which works to develop ethical and visionary leaders who will contribute to Africa’s growth and development.

About the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship

The Mandela Rhodes Scholarship is a postgraduate scholarship designed specifically for young African leaders who wish to pursue either an Honours degree or a Master’s degree at a recognized South African institution of higher education.

Unlike traditional scholarships that focus solely on financial support, the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship emphasizes holistic leadership development. Scholars participate in a structured leadership program built around the founding principles of reconciliation, education, entrepreneurship, and leadership.

These principles reflect the values of Nelson Mandela and aim to prepare young leaders to address complex social challenges while contributing to a more equitable and inclusive African continent.

The scholarship program combines academic study with leadership development workshops, mentorship, and personal growth opportunities.

Leadership Development Programme

The leadership development program is a central component of the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship experience. It is designed to challenge scholars intellectually and personally while equipping them with the tools needed to become effective leaders.

The program focuses on cultivating self-awareness, critical thinking, collaboration, and ethical leadership.

Scholars participate in a series of residential workshops held during their academic program. These workshops bring together scholars from across Africa to explore leadership challenges, exchange ideas, and develop solutions to real-world problems.

The program encourages scholars to engage in deep reflection, confront complex social issues, and develop the courage and competence required to lead change.

By combining academic learning with personal development, the program ensures that scholars graduate with both intellectual and leadership capabilities.

Financial Support and Scholarship Benefits

The Mandela Rhodes Scholarship provides comprehensive funding for the core costs associated with postgraduate studies in South Africa.

The scholarship covers tuition fees and university registration fees for the duration of the program. Scholars also receive financial support for study materials, research expenses, and medical aid.

Accommodation and meal costs are included as part of the scholarship package, ensuring that scholars can focus on their academic and leadership development.

In addition, scholars receive a personal allowance to cover basic living expenses while studying in South Africa.

The scholarship also covers economy class travel to and from the scholar’s home country to their South African university at the beginning and end of the academic program.

By covering these essential costs, the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship ensures that talented young Africans can pursue postgraduate education without financial barriers.

Duration of the Scholarship

The duration of the scholarship depends on the academic program undertaken.

Scholars pursuing an Honours degree receive funding for one year of study. Those pursuing a Master’s degree receive funding for up to two years.

During this time, scholars are required to participate fully in the leadership development workshops and other program activities organized by the Mandela Rhodes Foundation.

Fields of Study

The Mandela Rhodes Scholarship supports postgraduate study in any academic field.

Scholars may pursue programs in disciplines such as social sciences, engineering, business, law, education, health sciences, environmental studies, and many others.

Applicants must secure admission to a recognized South African university offering the relevant Honours or Master’s degree program.

Leadership Values and Selection Criteria

The Mandela Rhodes Foundation selects scholars based on four key leadership principles.

Leadership is a core attribute sought in applicants. The program looks for individuals who aspire to become visionary leaders with moral authority and who are committed to advancing equality, freedom, human rights, and human dignity.

Reconciliation is another important principle. Scholars are expected to demonstrate a commitment to understanding diverse perspectives and working toward a more just and inclusive society.

Education is equally important in the selection process. Applicants should have strong academic results and demonstrate a belief that education should be used as a tool for societal transformation.

Entrepreneurship is also emphasized. The foundation seeks individuals who demonstrate innovation, initiative, and a willingness to create new opportunities for themselves and others.

Eligibility Criteria

The Mandela Rhodes Scholarship is open to young African citizens who meet the academic and leadership criteria of the program.

Applicants must be citizens of an African country and must be between the ages of 19 and 29 at the time of application.

Candidates must have completed an undergraduate degree or be in the process of completing one with excellent academic results, typically above 70 percent or an upper-second-class Honours equivalent.

Applicants must also be able to study at the postgraduate level in English, as all participating universities conduct their programs in English.

Studying in South Africa

Scholars are expected to enroll in postgraduate programs at accredited South African universities.

Applicants are responsible for applying to their chosen universities and ensuring that they meet the entry requirements of their selected programs.

Applicants from outside South Africa may be required to complete verification of their academic qualifications through the South African Qualifications Authority.

Personal Development and Leadership Growth

One of the defining features of the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship is its focus on inner transformation.

The program encourages scholars to engage in personal reflection, examine their values, and develop self-awareness.

Participants learn to navigate complexity, embrace diversity, and collaborate with individuals from different cultural and social backgrounds.

The program creates a supportive environment where scholars can develop both their intellectual abilities and their leadership identity.

Alumni Impact and Testimonials

Over the years, the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship has helped develop a network of African leaders who are making significant contributions in fields such as education, public service, entrepreneurship, and social development.

Former scholars often highlight the transformative nature of the program.

Many alumni emphasize that the experience provided them with the space to explore their leadership values, connect with like-minded changemakers, and develop the confidence to pursue their goals.

The strong alumni network continues to support scholars long after they complete the program.

Application Process

Applicants must submit their applications online through the Mandela Rhodes Foundation application platform.

The application process includes completing an online form, providing academic transcripts and supporting documents, and submitting references from individuals who can speak to the applicant’s leadership potential and character.

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their materials early and to contact referees in advance to ensure timely submission of recommendation letters.

Application Deadline

Applications for the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship 2027 will close on 14 April 2026.

Interested applicants should begin preparing their applications as early as possible to ensure they meet all requirements before the deadline.

Application Link

Applicants can begin their application through the official portal.

Click HERE to learn more about the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship 2027.

Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Mandela Rhodes Scholarship 2027: Fully Funded Postgraduate Scholarship for African Students to Study in South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Carnegie Young Leaders for Civic Preparedness 2026: Up to $7,500 Funding for Youth-Led Civic Projects in the United States
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Carnegie Young Leaders for Civic Preparedness 2026 program, an ambitious national initiative designed to empower young people to lead meaningful civic change in their communities. The program, launched by the Institute for Citizens & Scholars with support from the Carnegie Corporation of New York, aims to mobilize a new […]

The post Carnegie Young Leaders for Civic Preparedness 2026: Up to $7,500 Funding for Youth-Led Civic Projects in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Carnegie Young Leaders for Civic Preparedness 2026 program, an ambitious national initiative designed to empower young people to lead meaningful civic change in their communities.

The program, launched by the Institute for Citizens & Scholars with support from the Carnegie Corporation of New York, aims to mobilize a new generation of young civic leaders who are ready to tackle pressing community challenges through collaboration, evidence-based problem solving, and inclusive dialogue.

Each year, the initiative selects approximately 100 youth-led teams consisting of five members each, bringing together a total of 500 young leaders from across the United States. These teams receive funding, mentorship, and professional development support to implement civic projects that address real challenges within their communities.

Selected teams can receive up to $7,500 in funding along with coaching, networking opportunities, and a full year of program support to bring their ideas to life and create measurable community impact.

The program is part of a broader effort to spark a nationwide movement of civic problem-solvers and aims to support 20 million young civic innovators by 2029.

About the Carnegie Young Leaders for Civic Preparedness Program

Carnegie Young Leaders for Civic Preparedness is a youth empowerment program focused on strengthening civic engagement and leadership among young people aged 14 to 24.

The program recognizes that young people are already leading important efforts to address social and community challenges. By providing resources, mentorship, and a national peer network, the initiative helps young changemakers transform their ideas into impactful community-driven solutions.

Participants develop three essential civic skills throughout the fellowship experience. These include the ability to engage in productive conversations across differences, the ability to identify and use credible information to understand complex issues, and the capacity to collaborate with others to design and implement solutions.

Through these skills, participants not only work on their own projects but also contribute to strengthening civic trust and problem-solving capacity within their communities.

Program Impact and Vision

The Carnegie Young Leaders initiative is designed to create a large-scale movement of civic leadership among young people.

The program brings together youth from diverse geographic, political, and cultural backgrounds across the United States. Participants work together to address challenges that affect their communities, ranging from public health and environmental sustainability to misinformation, civic participation, education, and economic opportunity.

The inaugural class of Carnegie Young Leaders includes 500 participants working on projects supported by an initial investment of $2.05 million from the Carnegie Corporation of New York.

By supporting youth-led initiatives, the program seeks to strengthen democratic participation and encourage collaborative approaches to solving societal problems.

Click HERE to download the details and FAQ.

Funding and Support for Fellows

Each selected team receives a financial stipend of up to $7,500 to support the implementation of their civic project.

This funding recognizes the value of the participants’ time and effort while providing the resources needed to execute their community initiatives.

In addition to financial support, fellows receive:

Personalized coaching from experienced mentors and community leaders.

Access to hands-on training and learning sessions designed to strengthen civic leadership skills.

Participation in a national peer network of young changemakers working on similar challenges.

Membership in a Community of Practice where fellows exchange ideas, share experiences, and learn from each other.

The program encourages participants to develop innovative solutions rooted in their lived experiences and the specific needs of their communities.

Types of Civic Projects

Applicants are encouraged to propose projects that address a wide range of community issues.

Possible areas of focus include climate change, public health, education, economic opportunity, community well-being, misinformation, democratic participation, and food security.

Projects should aim to reach at least 75 individuals through in-person engagement or 300 participants through online activities.

The program is particularly interested in initiatives that bring people together across differences, encourage dialogue and understanding, and use credible evidence to inform decision-making.

Click HERE to nominate a young person to apply.

Team Structure

Applicants must apply as part of a team consisting of five members.

This team-based approach encourages collaboration, peer learning, and shared leadership throughout the program.

Working in teams allows participants to develop stronger project ideas and ensures that responsibilities are distributed effectively among group members.

Eligibility Criteria

The program is open to young people aged 14 to 24 who are interested in leading community-based civic initiatives.

Applicants are encouraged to come from diverse racial, cultural, and geographic backgrounds. The program places particular emphasis on supporting young people from communities where civic leadership opportunities may be limited.

Both new and experienced changemakers are welcome to apply.

Participants do not need to have extensive prior experience in civic engagement, but they should demonstrate motivation, curiosity, and commitment to making a positive difference in their communities.

Learning and Leadership Development

Throughout the program, fellows engage in leadership development experiences designed to strengthen their ability to collaborate and communicate effectively.

Participants learn how to facilitate productive conversations, gather and evaluate credible information, and work with community members to design solutions that address real challenges.

These skills help participants build confidence and strengthen their capacity to lead civic initiatives both during and beyond the program.

Community and Networking Opportunities

Participants become part of a national network of young civic leaders from across the United States.

This network includes young people from urban and rural communities, different political perspectives, and diverse cultural backgrounds.

The program provides opportunities for fellows to connect, exchange ideas, and collaborate on solutions that extend beyond their own communities.

Through these interactions, participants gain exposure to a wide range of perspectives and experiences that enrich their leadership development.

Examples of Youth-Led Impact

Carnegie Young Leaders have already launched projects that demonstrate the power of youth-driven civic engagement.

One example is a project led by Karrington Harried, who created a program that pairs students with seniors in retirement homes to build intergenerational connections and reduce loneliness among older adults.

Another initiative organized by Patricia Mathu brings community members together for discussions on agriculture and food systems, encouraging dialogue between individuals with different political perspectives.

These projects illustrate how small actions can create meaningful change and strengthen community relationships.

Application Deadline

The priority deadline for applications is March 30, 2026.

Interested applicants should begin preparing their project proposals and assembling their teams as early as possible.

Application Link

Applicants can begin their application through the Official Program Portal.

Click HERE to learn more about the Carnegie Young Leaders for Civic Preparedness 2026 program.

Discover more global grants on OFY HERE.

The post Carnegie Young Leaders for Civic Preparedness 2026: Up to $7,500 Funding for Youth-Led Civic Projects in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Call: Women Digital Leadership Award 2026 Recognising Women Innovators in AI
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
In celebration of International Women’s Day 2026, the GSMA Asia Pacific (APAC) has officially opened submissions for the Women Digital Leadership Award 2026. This prestigious award recognizes outstanding women driving innovation, leadership, and impact in AI and digital technology across the Asia-Pacific region. The award will be presented during M360 ASEAN 2026 on 10 September […]

The post Global Call: Women Digital Leadership Award 2026 Recognising Women Innovators in AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In celebration of International Women’s Day 2026, the GSMA Asia Pacific (APAC) has officially opened submissions for the Women Digital Leadership Award 2026. This prestigious award recognizes outstanding women driving innovation, leadership, and impact in AI and digital technology across the Asia-Pacific region.

The award will be presented during M360 ASEAN 2026 on 10 September 2026 at the Grand Hyatt Kuala Lumpur. The initiative highlights women shaping the future of technology while addressing persistent challenges such as gender under-representation and algorithmic bias in AI systems.

Women, Trust, and the New AI Reality

The 2026 edition of the award focuses on the theme “Women, Trust, and the New AI Reality.” As artificial intelligence becomes central to telecom networks, cybersecurity, digital services, and customer safety, the role of women in shaping responsible and inclusive technology is more important than ever.

According to Christina Cheung, women across Asia-Pacific are building and managing some of the most sophisticated digital systems—from fraud detection models to advanced telecom networks—yet their expertise is still often questioned or overlooked.

Despite these challenges, women continue to lead transformative innovations that improve digital safety, strengthen network resilience, and create inclusive digital services.

Highlights from the 2025 Winners

The 2025 Women Digital Leadership Award celebrated inspiring women who are redefining digital leadership in the region. Some of the notable winners included:

  • Tara Neal – Recognized for insights on AI ethics, digital trust, and gender bias in technology.

  • Muntaha Asim – Celebrated for championing women’s voices in digital safety and online protection.

  • Katherine Ng – Honored for advancing responsible technology solutions for SMEs.

  • Chanita Craythorne – Acknowledged for highlighting online harms affecting women and girls.

Their work demonstrated how women are shaping safer and more inclusive digital ecosystems.

What Submissions Are Being Invited in 2026?

GSMA APAC is inviting thought-provoking contributions that explore the relationship between women, AI, and trust in the digital world.

Accepted Formats

Applicants can submit their entries in different formats, including:

  • Articles

  • Research insights

  • Case studies

  • Short videos

  • Podcasts

  • Personal reflections from work in AI, telecom networks, or digital policy

Suggested Topics

Some suggested themes include:

  • AI solutions that create safer online spaces for women

  • Addressing gaps in current digital systems

  • Job impacts and opportunities in the AI-driven economy

  • Women-led innovations in digital services

  • Real-world experiences in deploying or regulating AI in APAC

Applicants are also encouraged to submit original perspectives or experiences, even if they fall outside the suggested topics.

Benefits of Applying

Winning or being shortlisted for the Women Digital Leadership Award offers several valuable opportunities:

  • Global recognition for leadership in AI and digital innovation

  • Publication on the GSMA APAC #ChangeTheFace blog

  • Networking opportunities with global telecom and digital leaders

  • Recognition at the Digital Nation Awards during M360 ASEAN 2026

  • Increased visibility for women shaping the future of technology

The award celebrates insight, impact, and leadership—not job titles.

Recognition at M360 ASEAN 2026

Top entries will be featured during the Digital Nation Awards ceremony at M360 ASEAN 2026 in Kuala Lumpur. The recognition event will take place on 10 September 2026 at the Grand Hyatt Kuala Lumpur.

Selected submissions will also be published on the GSMA APAC #ChangeTheFace platform, amplifying the voices of women leading digital transformation across the region.

Deadline for Applications

Application Deadline: 31 May 2026

Applicants or nominators should submit their entries directly to Billy Chan at GSMA APAC.

For more details about this application, click Here.

The Women Digital Leadership Award 2026 is part of a broader effort by GSMA to ensure that women play a leading role in the development, governance, and deployment of artificial intelligence and digital technologies across Asia-Pacific.

If you are a woman shaping the future of technology—or know someone who is—this is an opportunity to share your story and inspire the next generation of digital leaders.

For more information please click here. 

The post Global Call: Women Digital Leadership Award 2026 Recognising Women Innovators in AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: UNICEF Education Specialist (UN Volunteer) in Lao PDR – 2026 International Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Are you passionate about improving education systems and empowering young learners worldwide? The United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) is seeking a qualified Education Specialist to join its Education Programme in Lao People’s Democratic Republic. This international volunteer role, hosted through the United Nations Volunteers programme, offers professionals the chance to contribute to strengthening foundational learning […]

The post Apply Now: UNICEF Education Specialist (UN Volunteer) in Lao PDR – 2026 International Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about improving education systems and empowering young learners worldwide? The United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) is seeking a qualified Education Specialist to join its Education Programme in Lao People’s Democratic Republic. This international volunteer role, hosted through the United Nations Volunteers programme, offers professionals the chance to contribute to strengthening foundational learning and skills development for children and adolescents.

The position will be based in Vientiane and supports UNICEF’s ongoing education initiatives under the UNICEF Country Programme 2022–2026, while also contributing to the transition toward the 2027–2031 programme cycle.

About the Opportunity

The Education Specialist will support UNICEF’s mission to advance children’s rights and improve education outcomes across Lao PDR. The country currently faces significant economic challenges that have impacted the education sector, leading to declining school enrollment rates and a rise in out-of-school children.

By joining the UNICEF Education team, the selected volunteer will help strengthen foundational literacy, numeracy, and essential life skills while promoting equitable access to quality education for the most disadvantaged children and adolescents.

This opportunity directly contributes to Sustainable Development Goal 4.

Key Assignment Details

  • Position: Education Specialist (International UN Volunteer)

  • Host Organization: United Nations Children’s Fund

  • Location: Vientiane

  • Country: Lao People’s Democratic Republic

  • Work Modality: Onsite

  • Duration: 9 months (with possibility of extension)

  • Start Date: 6 April 2026

  • Volunteer Category: International Specialist

  • Work Arrangement: Full-time

Why This Opportunity Matters

This assignment provides a unique opportunity to contribute to transformative change in education in Lao PDR. By supporting UNICEF’s education initiatives, the selected volunteer will help expand learning opportunities, strengthen education systems, and ensure children—especially the most disadvantaged—have the skills they need to succeed.

Key Responsibilities

The Education Specialist will work under the supervision of the Chief of Education and collaborate closely with government partners, development organizations, and education stakeholders. Key responsibilities include:

1. Support Foundational Learning and Skills Development

  • Assist with planning, implementation, monitoring, and reporting of education programme activities.

  • Strengthen literacy, numeracy, and skills development initiatives for children and adolescents.

  • Promote innovative learning approaches including digital and technology-based education tools.

  • Support teacher training, school leadership development, and strategies to prevent school dropout.

2. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Knowledge Management

  • Contribute to data collection, sector analysis, and education research.

  • Support programme monitoring, evaluation, and evidence-based reporting.

  • Document best practices and lessons learned to strengthen education policy and programming.

3. Education Sector Coordination and Partnerships

  • Support coordination within the Education and Sports Sector Working Group and UN Education Working Group.

  • Facilitate collaboration between government institutions, development partners, NGOs, and private sector stakeholders.

  • Assist in expanding partnerships, including initiatives supported by the Global Partnership for Education.

  • Support donor reporting, resource mobilization, and advocacy efforts.

4. Additional Programme Support

  • Contribute to annual planning, programme reviews, and funding proposals.

  • Assist with coordination of donor visits and programme documentation.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Education: Master’s degree in Education, Social Sciences, International Development, or related fields.

  • Experience: Minimum 3 years of relevant professional experience in education, development programmes, or social policy.

  • Language: Fluency in English.

  • Nationality: Must be a national of a country other than Lao PDR.

Desirable Experience

  • Experience working with international organizations such as the UN.

  • Experience working with government institutions in developing countries.

  • Knowledge of foundational learning, education systems strengthening, or digital learning initiatives.

Required Competencies

Successful candidates should demonstrate:

  • Accountability and professionalism

  • Strong communication and teamwork skills

  • Adaptability and cultural sensitivity

  • Planning and organizational abilities

  • Commitment to diversity, ethics, and integrity

  • Technological awareness and innovation mindset

Benefits and Allowances

Selected UN Volunteers will receive several benefits, including:

  • Volunteer Living Allowance: USD 2,654.55 per month

  • Entry Lump Sum: USD 3,500

  • Exit allowance at the end of assignment

  • Family allowance (if applicable)

  • Health and life insurance coverage

Additional support includes travel arrangements for official missions and integration into the UNICEF and UN Volunteer network in the country.

Living Conditions in Lao PDR

The role will be based in Vientiane, a safe family duty station located along the Mekong River. The city offers a range of accommodation options, with rents typically ranging from USD 500 to USD 1,500 per month depending on property size and location.

International schools, childcare services, and expatriate support networks are available, making the duty station suitable for professionals relocating with families.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply through the official United Nations Volunteers platform.

Deadline: 16 March 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early, as positions may close once suitable candidates are identified.

Discover more opportunities Here.

The post Apply Now: UNICEF Education Specialist (UN Volunteer) in Lao PDR – 2026 International Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Powerful Social Enterprise Startup Program 2026: All Good Ventures 12-Month Support (Seed Funding + Mentorship)
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
Applications are now open for the All Good Ventures Startup Support Programme 2026, a powerful 12-month programme designed to support early-stage social entrepreneurs building businesses that improve the lives of people facing extreme hardship. The programme provides seed funding, one-to-one mentoring, and practical startup support to help founders launch and grow impactful social enterprises. Entrepreneurs […]

The post Powerful Social Enterprise Startup Program 2026: All Good Ventures 12-Month Support (Seed Funding + Mentorship) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the All Good Ventures Startup Support Programme 2026, a powerful 12-month programme designed to support early-stage social entrepreneurs building businesses that improve the lives of people facing extreme hardship.

The programme provides seed funding, one-to-one mentoring, and practical startup support to help founders launch and grow impactful social enterprises.

Entrepreneurs from anywhere in the world can apply, making this an excellent opportunity for founders developing businesses focused on solving critical social challenges.

Applications are open until 31 March 2026.

About the All Good Ventures Programme

All Good Ventures supports founders who are building social enterprises that generate income while creating meaningful social impact.

Unlike traditional charities or non-profit programs, All Good Ventures focuses on businesses that generate profits while reinvesting those profits to improve the lives of marginalized communities.

The programme particularly supports founders who are still in the early stages of building their businesses, helping them turn promising ideas into sustainable enterprises.

Through mentorship, financial support, and business guidance, participants receive the tools and resources needed to develop strong business models and launch successful ventures.

Programme Benefits

Successful applicants receive a range of support designed to help their social enterprises grow and become financially sustainable.

Benefits include:

  • Seed funding grant to support startup development
  • One-to-one business mentoring
  • Practical guidance on building a business plan and budget
  • Monthly mentorship meetings with experienced advisors
  • Access to a global network of social entrepreneurs
  • Long-term support during the early growth phase

The programme focuses on both business sustainability and social impact, ensuring that enterprises can create lasting change.

Programme Duration

The All Good Ventures programme runs for 12 months.

During this period, founders work closely with mentors to refine their business models, develop operational strategies, and strengthen their impact plans.

Successful applicants also participate in regular mentoring sessions and receive ongoing guidance as they grow their enterprises.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the programme, applicants must meet the following criteria:

1. Your Business Must Be a Social Enterprise

The programme supports businesses with a social mission, not non-profit organizations.

A social enterprise is a business that generates profits and uses those profits to support a specific social cause.

2. Early-Stage Business

Applicants must be in the pre-seed, seed, or early stage of development.

Most successful applicants are within the first two years of launching their business.

3. Financial Sustainability Potential

The enterprise must have a business model that aims to become financially sustainable over time.

This means the business should eventually generate enough revenue to cover operational costs and salaries.

4. Focus on Helping People in Need

The programme supports enterprises that aim to improve the lives of marginalized communities facing extreme hardship.

Target beneficiaries may include individuals lacking access to:

  • Food and water
  • Shelter
  • Healthcare
  • Education
  • Safety
  • Employment opportunities

Enterprises focused primarily on environmental issues are not eligible.

5. Commitment to the 12-Month Programme

Applicants must commit to participating in the full year-long mentorship programme.

This includes developing a business plan, preparing financial projections, and attending monthly mentoring sessions.

6. Time Commitment

Applicants must demonstrate they can dedicate at least 20 hours per week to developing their enterprise, with the goal of eventually working on the venture full-time.

Application Requirements

The application process requires:

  • Written responses about the business idea
  • A short video submission
  • Applications submitted in English

Applicants should note that AI-generated responses or videos are not accepted. The organization wants to hear directly from founders in their own words.

Programme Requirements for Selected Applicants

If selected, participants must:

  • Have a strong working knowledge of English
  • Have reliable internet and computer access
  • Be comfortable using Microsoft Word and Excel
  • Provide updates and progress reports on their projects

Since the programme is delivered online, founders from anywhere in the world can participate.

Selection Timeline

After submitting an application, the process follows this timeline:

  • April – Early May: Application review and shortlist notifications
  • May: Final selection and support agreements signed
  • June: Seed funding released and mentorship programme begins

Application Deadline

📅 31 March 2026

(11:59 PM NZT)

Official Website

Applicants can learn more and apply through the official website

APPLY HERE

For more exciting opportunities including scholarships, fellowships, internships, and grants, click here.

 

The post Powerful Social Enterprise Startup Program 2026: All Good Ventures 12-Month Support (Seed Funding + Mentorship) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Magic Grants 2026–2027 Offering Up to $300,000 for Media Innovation Projects
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Applications are now open for the 2026–2027 Magic Grants offered by the Brown Institute for Media Innovation—a collaboration between Stanford School of Engineering and the Columbia Journalism School. Each year, the institute awards over $1 million in grants to support groundbreaking projects that combine technology and storytelling. The Magic Grant program encourages innovators to experiment […]

The post Apply Now: Magic Grants 2026–2027 Offering Up to $300,000 for Media Innovation Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the 2026–2027 Magic Grants offered by the Brown Institute for Media Innovation—a collaboration between Stanford School of Engineering and the Columbia Journalism School.

Each year, the institute awards over $1 million in grants to support groundbreaking projects that combine technology and storytelling. The Magic Grant program encourages innovators to experiment with new tools, new stories, and new forms of media, helping shape the future of journalism and digital storytelling.

Successful projects often bring together journalists, engineers, computer scientists, designers, and digital humanities scholars to develop creative solutions that improve how stories are discovered, produced, and shared.

Grant Benefits

Selected teams will receive a comprehensive package designed to support both the development and impact of their projects:

  • Funding up to $150,000 for one year

  • Up to $300,000 for “bicoastal” teams with members from both Stanford and Columbia

  • Salaries and fellowships for team members

  • Funding for software, hardware, prototyping, production, and services

  • Professional development opportunities, including entrepreneurship training

  • Access to expert mentors and advisors

  • Membership in a global alumni network of innovators

Since its launch, the program has supported 150+ projects and nearly 400 innovators working at the intersection of media and technology.

Types of Projects Supported

Magic Grants support a wide range of innovative initiatives, including:

  • New journalism storytelling formats

  • AI tools for audio and video editing

  • Data visualization and immersive storytelling

  • Machine learning applications in media

  • New software platforms and database tools for journalism

  • Speech analysis and data-sharing technologies

Projects can range from software platforms and research initiatives to multimedia storytelling and hardware prototypes.

Eligibility

The Magic Grant program welcomes proposals from:

  • Students

  • Faculty

  • Alumni

  • Practitioners working in media and technology

Applicants must have connections to either Stanford University or Columbia University.

The program especially encourages “bicoastal” collaborations involving team members from both institutions.

Evaluation Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on four main factors:

  1. Originality and innovation of the project

  2. Potential impact

  3. Strength and expertise of the team

  4. Feasibility and timeline for completion

Applicants should clearly outline their project concept, timeline, expected outcomes, and potential challenges.

Application Process

Applications must be submitted through the Submittable platform and typically include:

  • Project summary and detailed description

  • Timeline and milestones

  • Explanation of innovation and impact

  • Discussion of potential challenges and risks

  • Team member details and CVs

  • Budget (up to $150,000 for 12 months)

  • Optional supporting documents and letters of support

Finalists will be invited to submit a 5-minute presentation video and participate in a virtual Q&A session before the final selection.

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: April 1, 2026 (11:59 PM PT)

  • Finalists Announced: April 10, 2026

  • Stanford Finalist Presentations: April 24, 2026

  • Columbia Finalist Presentations: April 27, 2026

  • Winners Announced: April 30, 2026

  • Project Start Date: September 2026 (or Summer 2026 by arrangement)

Information Sessions

Interested applicants can attend information sessions hosted at Stanford and Columbia, including virtual sessions to learn more about the program and application process.

Discover more Grant Opportunities Here.

 

The post Apply Now: Magic Grants 2026–2027 Offering Up to $300,000 for Media Innovation Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
